title: 2026 Where would you start
author: celzo1776
selftext: Seeing all these post with 6-7 digit accounts I want to do a real life experiment with my own money in play. Starting with 200$ and adding the same amount to a trading account for the next 12 months I need your inputs to get this growing, not Wild Wild West rando model trading but using proven strategies, so where do I start?

comments.author: littleDevX
comments.body:
Dont forget: almost every 6-7 fig account post is a scam. Don’t pay attention to these posts.

1) find a good trading platform, which allows you to create an api wallet (I use hyperliquid)
2) define filters for your agent/bot (hardest part), but if you have no idea start brainstorming with any llm.
3) let an agent/bot (which you create by yourself - best case) execute these trades on your behalf.

Its a super interesting journey and it can be rewarding, but depending on your background it can be very challenging.

Good luck mate

replies.author: celzo1776
replies.body: Thanks for you feedback, scamcity is open for buisness that is for sure :)) I have been plowing thru GitHub and found some interesting projects that will get my attention, I have dev/tech/ai/finance background but want to build this as a project for others to follow, that is why i am seeking inputs for starting points

comments.author: gabrigoo
comments.body: I’ve been using this paper money agent for a couple of weeks just to test how things are in the space. Maybe worth a look, tryinvesti.co/u/gabrigoo

comments.author: celzo1776
comments.body: Little update, those who are dm'ing me with links to the most revolutionery app or site based on Kalshi and PM I guess you didn't read the bottom half of the post.

comments.author: Forsaken_Leader_8
comments.body: starting with 200 is a smart move to learn the psychology without blowing up your life. for the "proven strategy" part, i've found that having an objective second opinion helps a ton. i usually check signalwhisper to validate my setups before i put any cash on them. it gives a good technical confidence score so you aren't just guessing on rando models like you mentioned.
